<<Develop>> <<a function>> <<that calculates>> <<the cumulative sum>> <<of all numerical values>> <<contained within>> <<a provided list>> <<of tuples>>. <<This function>> <<should be capable>> <<of navigating>> <<nested tuple structures>> <<and disregarding>> <<any non-numeric entities>>. <<It must recursively>> <<process each element>> <<to ensure>> <<every numeric value>> <<is accounted for>> <<in the final sum>>. <<The function>> <<must be written>> <<in a way>> <<that handles>> <<various levels of nesting>> <<and ignores>> <<non-numeric data types>> <<such as strings or booleans>>. <<This ensures>> <<the function>> <<is robust>> <<and versatile>> <<for different input structures>>.

******

<<The first approach>> <<involves using recursion>> <<to traverse>> <<each tuple>> <<and its nested elements>>. <<A helper function>> <<is created>> <<to check if an element is numeric>> <<and add it to the sum if it is>>. <<This helper function>> <<is called recursively>> <<for each item in the tuple>> <<to handle nested structures>>. <<The main function>> <<initializes the sum at zero>> <<and calls the helper function>> <<on the input list of tuples>>. <<This method>> <<ensures that all numeric values>> <<are captured>> <<and summed>> <<regardless of their depth in the nested structure>>. <<It is efficient>> <<and adheres to the requirement>> <<of ignoring non-numeric entities>>.

******

<<The second approach>> <<uses a stack to manage the traversal>> <<of nested tuples>>. <<Each element is pushed onto the stack>> <<and processed in a loop>>. <<If an element is a tuple>> <<it is further broken down>> <<and its elements are added to the stack>>. <<If the element is numeric>> <<it is added to the cumulative sum>>. <<This approach>> <<avoids recursion>> <<and is suitable for very deep nesting>> <<where recursion may lead to stack overflow>>. <<It also ensures>> <<non-numeric entities>> <<are ignored>> <<and the sum is calculated correctly>>. <<The stack-based method>> <<provides an alternative>> <<way to handle nested structures>> <<without using recursion>> <<and is equally effective>>.

******

<<The function must handle>> <<different data types>> <<within the tuples
